blocks:
Surah Muhammad (peace be upon him) and it is also called Surah al-Qital (The Fighting). It is Madaniyyah (Medinan).

Its number of verses is forty according to the Basran count, and thirty-eight according to the Kufan count. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ أَضَلَّ أَعْمالَهُمْ (1) (As for) those who disbelieve and bar (others) from the way of Allah, He shall render their works void.

Al-Qummi narrates: It was revealed concerning the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله who apostatized after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, usurped the right of His Household (أهل بيته), and barred (people) from the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and from the Wilayah (guardianship/authority) of the Imams عليهم السلام. "He shall render their works void" (أَضَلَّ أَعْمالَهُمْ) means He nullified what they had previously done alongside the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله in terms of Jihad (striving) and support (النصرة).

And narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said: The Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said in the mosque, after the passing of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, while the people were gathered, in a loud voice: الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ أَضَلَّ أَعْمالَهُمْ (As for) those who disbelieve and bar (others) from the way of Allah, He shall render their works void.

Ibn Abbas said to him: "O Abu al-Hasan, why did you say what you said?" He replied: "I recited something from the Quran." Ibn Abbas said: "You surely said it for a reason." He said: "Yes. Indeed, Allah says in His Book: وَما آتاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَما نَهاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا (And take whatever the Messenger gives you, and refrain from whatever he forbids you) Do you testify concerning the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله that He appointed Abu Bakr as successor?" Ibn Abbas said: "I did not hear the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله entrust the succession to anyone except you." He (Ali) said: "Then why did you not pledge allegiance to me?" Ibn Abbas replied: "The people gathered upon Abu Bakr, and I was among them."

Then the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said: "Just as the people of the Calf gathered upon the Calf, here you have caused sedition (فتنتم). And your likeness is: كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْقَدَ ناراً فَلَمَّا أَضاءَتْ ما حَوْلَهُ ذَهَبَ اللهُ بِنُورِهِمْ وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِي ظُلُماتٍ لا يُبْصِرُونَ صُمٌّ بُكْمٌ عُمْيٌ فَهُمْ لا يَرْجِعُونَ (Like the case of someone who kindled a fire, but when it illuminated all around him, Allah took away their light and left them in darkness, so they do not see. Deaf, dumb, and blind, they will not return.)"

(٢) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ وَآمَنُوا بِما نُزِّلَ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ صلّى الله عليه وآله. (2) And those who have faith and do righteous deeds and believe in what has been sent down to Muhammad

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: "In what has been sent down to Muhammad (peace be upon him and his progeny) concerning Ali (peace be upon him)." This is how it was revealed: وَهُوَ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ كَفَّرَ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ وَأَصْلَحَ بالَهُمْ (2) and it is the truth from their Lord—He will absolve them of their misdeeds and set their hearts right.

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Abu Dharr, Salman, Ammar, and al-Miqdad, who did not break the covenant. He said: وَآمَنُوا بِما نُزِّلَ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ صلّى الله عليه وآله, meaning: they remained firm upon the Wilayah (الولاية) which Allah sent down. وَهُوَ الْحَقُ , meaning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). بالَهُمْ , meaning their condition (حالهم).

(٣) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا اتَّبَعُوا الْباطِلَ ** (3) That is because those who disbelieved followed falsehood He said: And they are those who followed the enemies of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), upon both of whom be the blessings of Allah. وَأَنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّبَعُوا الْحَقَّ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ كَذلِكَ يَضْرِبُ اللهُ لِلنَّاسِ أَمْثالَهُمْ.** and because those who believed followed the truth from their Lord. Thus does Allah draw their parables for mankind.

القمّيّ narrated from الصادق (peace be upon him) that He said: In Surat Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم there is a verse concerning us and a verse concerning our enemies.

(٤) فَإِذا لَقِيتُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (4) When you meet the faithless in warfare فَضَرْبَ الرِّقابِ ** strike the necks so strike the necks with a striking حَتَّى إِذا أَثْخَنْتُمُوهُمْ ** When you have thoroughly subdued them you have multiplied their killing and made it severe, derived from الثخين which is the thick/severe فَشُدُّوا الْوَثاقَ so take them captive and guard them. And الوثاق (with fathah and kasrah) is that by which one is bound فَإِمَّا مَنًّا بَعْدُ وَإِمَّا فِداءً ** then afterwards, either release them as a favour, or [take] ransom so either you grant a favor (منّاً) or you take a ransom (فداء). The intent is the choice after capture between granting a favor and releasing, and between taking ransom حَتَّى تَضَعَ الْحَرْبُ أَوْزارَها ** until the war lays down its burdens its tools and its heavy loads by which it cannot stand except through them, such as weapons (السلاح) and mounts (الكراع); meaning: the war ends, and none remains except a Muslim or one who has made peace (مسالم).

In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "My father used to say that war has two rulings (حكمين). If the war is ongoing, and it has not laid down its burdens, and its people have not been thoroughly subdued (يثخن), then every captive taken in that state—the Imam has a choice concerning him: if He wills, He strikes his neck; and if He wills, He cuts off his hand and foot on opposite sides without cauterization (بغير حسم) and leaves him to wallow in his blood until he dies. This is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنَّما جَزاءُ الَّذِينَ يُحارِبُونَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ** [the rest of] the Ayah." The punishment of those who wage war against Allah and His Apostle He said: "And the other ruling (الحكم الآخر) is when the war has laid down its burdens and its people have been thoroughly subdued (أثخن أهلها). Then every captive taken in that state, who is in their hands—the Imam has a choice concerning him: if He wills, He grants them a favor (منّ عليهم) and releases them; and if He wills, He ransoms them for their lives (فاداهم أنفسهم); and if He wills, He enslaves them, and they become slaves (عبيداً)."

ذلِكَ The command is that وَلَوْ يَشاءُ اللهُ لَانْتَصَرَ مِنْهُمْ ** Had Allah wished, He would have taken vengeance on them He would have taken vengeance on them by eradication وَلكِنْ لِيَبْلُوَا بَعْضَكُمْ بِبَعْضٍ but [He ordained fighting] that He may test some of you by means of others But He commanded you to fight so that He may test the believers by means of the disbelievers, by them striving against them (يجاهدوهم) so they may earn the great reward; and [He tests] the disbelievers by means of the believers, by hastening some of their punishment upon them at their hands, so that some of them may be deterred from disbelief وَالَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** As for those who are killed in the way of Allah Meaning: they strove [in Jihad]. And it was read قُتِلوا (passive), meaning: they were martyred فَلَنْ يُضِلَّ أَعْمالَهُمْ ** He will never let their works go to waste** He will never waste them.

(٥) سَيَهْدِيهِمْ ** He will guide them الى الجنة وَيُصْلِحُ بالَهُمْ to Paradise and set their minds right, (٦) وَيُدْخِلُهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ عَرَّفَها لَهُمْ ** and admit them into Paradise, which He has made known to them. Al-Qummi said: meaning, He promised it to them and reserved it for them. (٧) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنْ تَنْصُرُوا اللهَ ** O you who have faith! If you help Allah, if you help His religion, His Messenger, and the successor (وصيّ) of His Messenger, He will help you against your enemy وَيُثَبِّتْ أَقْدامَكُمْ ** and make your feet steady, in upholding the rights (حقوق) of Islam and striving (المجاهدة) against the disbelievers. (٨) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَتَعْساً لَهُمْ ** But as for those who are faithless, woe to them! meaning, stumbling and degradation (انحطاط) وَأَضَلَّ أَعْمالَهُمْ. and He has rendered their works abortive. (٩) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَرِهُوا ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ فَأَحْبَطَ أَعْمالَهُمْ. That is because they disliked what Allah has sent down, so He rendered their works abortive. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), who said: Gabriel descended upon Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with this verse thus: ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَرِهُوا ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ concerning Ali, except that the name (الاسم) was removed (كشط), فَأَحْبَطَ أَعْمالَهُمْ so He rendered their works abortive. And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: كَرِهُوا ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ ** concerning the right (حقّ) of Ali (peace be upon him).

(١٠) أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ دَمَّرَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ (10) Have they not travelled in the land so that they might observe how was the fate of those who were before them? Allah destroyed them.

Al-Qummi: Meaning, have they not looked into the accounts of the past nations, whom He destroyed and tormented? وَلِلْكافِرِينَ أَمْثالُها (And for the faithless there are similar fates). He said: Meaning those who disbelieved and hated what Allah revealed concerning Ali عليه السلام shall have the like of the torment and destruction that befell the past nations.

(١١) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللهَ مَوْلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (11) That is because Allah is the Guardian of those who have faith, (He is) their helper (ناصرهم) against their enemies. Al-Qummi said: meaning those who remained firm upon the Imamate (امامة) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). وَأَنَّ الْكافِرِينَ لا مَوْلى لَهُمْ and that the faithless have no guardian, who would repel the punishment (العذاب) from them. It was said: This does not contradict His saying, the Almighty: وَرُدُّوا إِلَى اللهِ مَوْلاهُمُ الْحَق ** and they will be returned to Allah, their true Guardian, for the al-Mawla (المولى) therein is in the meaning of the Owner (المالك).

(١٢) إِنَّ اللهَ يُدْخِلُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَتَمَتَّعُونَ ** (12) Indeed Allah will admit those who have faith and do righteous deeds into gardens with streams running in them. But those who are faithless enjoy themselves يَتَمَتَّعُونَ (they benefit from the provision of the world) وَيَأْكُلُونَ كَما تَأْكُلُ الْأَنْعامُ and eat as the cattle eat, being greedy (حريصين), heedless (غافلين) of the consequence (العاقبة). وَالنَّارُ مَثْوىً لَهُمْ ** and the Fire will be their abode. مَثْوىً لَهُمْ (a dwelling place (منزل) and a station (مقام)).

** وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ هِيَ أَشَدُّ قُوَّةً مِنْ قَرْيَتِكَ الَّتِي أَخْرَجَتْكَ أَهْلَكْناهُمْ ** (13) How many a town, mightier in power than your town which expelled you, did We destroy, with various kinds of punishment, فَلا ناصِرَ لَهُمْ ** (and they had no helper) who could ward it off from them. ** أَفَمَنْ كانَ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ. (14) Is he who stands on a manifest proof from his Lord Al-Qummi says: meaning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), كَمَنْ زُيِّنَ لَهُ سُوءُ عَمَلِهِ وَاتَّبَعُوا أَهْواءَهُمْ ** (like him whose evil deeds are made fair-seeming to him, and they follow their desires?) meaning those who usurped him. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the hypocrites (المنافقون).

(١٥) مَثَلُ الْجَنَّةِ meaning, the parable of the people of the Garden. And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): that He read أمثال الجنّة (the parables of the Garden) in the plural form. ** الَّتِي وُعِدَ الْمُتَّقُونَ فِيها أَنْهارٌ مِنْ ماءٍ غَيْرِ آسِنٍ ** (which the Godwary have been promised: in it are rivers of water unstaling,) whose taste and smell are not altered. And it was read اسِن. وَأَنْهارٌ مِنْ لَبَنٍ لَمْ يَتَغَيَّرْ طَعْمُهُ وَأَنْهارٌ مِنْ خَمْرٍ لَذَّةٍ لِلشَّارِبِينَ ** (and rivers of milk whose taste does not change, and rivers of wine delicious to the drinkers,) delicious, in which there is no unpleasantness (كراهة), nor smell (ريح), nor the ill effect (غائلة) of intoxication (سكر) or hangover (خمار). القمّيّ (said): When the friend (وليّ) of Allah partakes of it, he finds the scent of musk in it. وَأَنْهارٌ مِنْ عَسَلٍ مُصَفًّى ** (and rivers of purified honey.) which is not mixed with wax, the waste products (فضلات) of bees, or other things. وَلَهُمْ فِيها مِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَراتِ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ كَمَنْ هُوَ خالِدٌ فِي النَّارِ (and for them therein are all [kinds of] fruits and forgiveness from their Lord. Are they like those who will abide in the Fire) [Are they] like the likeness of one who is abiding eternally in the Fire? وَسُقُوا ماءً حَمِيماً ** (and will be given to drink boiling water) instead of those drinks. فَقَطَّعَ أَمْعاءَهُمْ ** (that will cut their intestines?) due to the extremity of the heat. القمّيّ said: He who is in this described Garden is not like him who is in this Fire, just as the enemy of Allah is not like His friend (وليّه). And narrated from his father (peace be upon him), raised (مرفوعاً), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "When I entered the Garden, I saw in the Garden the Ṭūbā tree, and a river flows at the root of that tree, from which the four rivers burst forth: a river مِنْ ماءٍ غَيْرِ آسِنٍ ** (of water unstaling,) up to His saying: مُصَفًّى. (purified honey.) And in al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, in a ḥadīth, he said: "There is no believer in the Garden except that he has many gardens, trellised (معروشات) and untrellised (وغير معروشات), and rivers of wine, and rivers of water, and rivers of milk, and rivers of honey."

(١٦)** وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَسْتَمِعُ إِلَيْكَ حَتَّى إِذا خَرَجُوا مِنْ عِنْدِكَ قالُوا لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ ما ذا قالَ آنِفاً ** (16) And among them are those who listen to you, until, when they leave your presence, they say to those who were given knowledge, 'What did he say just now?'

Al-Qummi (narrates): It was revealed concerning the hypocrites among the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and concerning those who, when they heard something, would not believe in it nor retain it (يَعِهِ). Then, when they left, they would say to the believers: "What did Muhammad say just now?"

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: "We were with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He would inform us of the revelation (الوحي). I would retain it (أَعِيهِ), and so would those who retained it. Then, when we left, قالُوا لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ ما ذا قالَ آنِفاً أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ طَبَعَ اللهُ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ وَاتَّبَعُوا أَهْواءَهُمْ." (they say to those who were given knowledge, 'What did he say just now?' (17) They are the ones whose hearts Allah has sealed, and who follow their [own] desires.)

Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to call his companions. Whoever Allah willed good for, he would hear and know what He was calling him to. And whoever Allah willed evil for, He sealed his heart, so he neither hears nor comprehends. And that is His Almighty saying: **أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ طَبَعَ اللهُ ** [up to] the end of the Ayah.

(١٧) وَالَّذِينَ اهْتَدَوْا زادَهُمْ هُدىً وَآتاهُمْ تَقْواهُمْ. (17) But as for those who are guided, He enhances their guidance and gives them their God-wariness. (١٨) فَهَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا السَّاعَةَ ** Do they await anything other than it ** أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمْ بَغْتَةً فَقَدْ جاءَ أَشْراطُها ** Its signs (أماراتها) have already appeared. ** فَأَنَّى لَهُمْ إِذا جاءَتْهُمْ ذِكْراهُمْ ** Their remembrance (تذكرهم) will not benefit them at that time, nor will they have any respite (فراغ).

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was asked about the Hour, and He said: "It will be when there is belief in the stars (النجوم) and denial of Divine Decree (القدر)."

In al-Ilal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, in the answers to the questions of Abdullah ibn Salam: "As for the signs (أشراط) of the Hour, they are a fire that will gather mankind from the East to the West."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Among the signs (أشراط) of the Hour is that paralysis (الفالج) and sudden death (موت الفجأة) will become widespread."

In Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "Indeed, among the signs (أشراط) of the Hour is that knowledge (العلم) will be lifted, ignorance (الجهل) will appear, wine (الخمر) will be drunk, fornication (الزنا) will become widespread, men will decrease, and women will increase, until fifty women will have only one man among them."

And al-Qummi, narrated from Ibn Abbas, who said: We performed the Farewell Pilgrimage (حجّة الوداع) with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. He took hold of the ring of the door of the Ka'bah, then turned His face towards us and said: "Shall I not inform you of the signs (أشراط) of the Hour?" The person closest to Him that day was Salman (may Allah have mercy upon him). Salman said: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "Indeed, among the signs (أشراط) of the Resurrection (القيامة) are the squandering of prayers (الصلوات), the following of desires (الشهوات), inclining towards whims (الأهواء), the glorification of the wealthy (أصحاب المال), and the selling of religion (الدين) for the world (الدنيا). At that time, the heart of the believer will melt within his chest, just as salt melts in water, because of the evil (المنكر) he sees and is unable to change." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! Indeed, at that time, they will be ruled by oppressive rulers (أمراء جورة), wicked ministers (وزراء فَسَقَة), tyrannical chiefs (عرفاء ظلمة), and treacherous trustees (أمناء خونة)." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! Indeed, at that time, the evil (المنكر) will become known (معروفا), and the known (المعروف) will become evil (منكراً). The treacherous will be trusted, and the trustworthy will be betrayed. The liar will be believed, and the truthful will be denied." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, there will be the rule (امارة) of women, the consultation of slave-girls (الإماء), and the seating of boys upon the pulpits (المنابر). Lying (الكذب) will be considered cleverness (ظرفاً), Zakat (الزكاة) will be considered a penalty (مغرماً), and the spoils of war (الفيء) will be considered a gain (مغنماً). A man will treat his parents (والديه) harshly but be kind to his friend, and the tailed star (الكوكب المذنب) will appear." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! And at that time, the woman will partner with her husband in trade, and the rain will be scarce (غيضاً), causing the noble ones (الكرام) to be enraged (غيضاً). The destitute man (المعسر) will be scorned. At that time, the markets will be close together. One will say, 'I have sold nothing,' and another will say, 'I have made no profit,' so you will see none except those who blame Allah." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, they will be ruled by people who, if they speak, they will kill them, and if they remain silent, they will seize their possessions (استباحوهم). They will monopolize their spoils (فيئهم), violate their sanctity (حرمتهم), shed their blood, and fill their hearts with malice (دغلاً) and terror (رعباً). You will not see them except fearful, afraid, terrified, and intimidated." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! Indeed, at that time, something will come from the East and something from the West, ruling over my Ummah. Woe to the weak among my Ummah because of them, and woe to them from Allah! They will not show mercy to the young, nor respect the old, nor refrain from harming the evildoer. Their bodies are the bodies of humans (الآدميّين), but their hearts are the hearts of devils (الشياطين)." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! And at that time, men will suffice themselves with men, and women with women. Boys (الغلمان) will be guarded jealously, just as a girl (الجارية) is guarded in her family's house. Men will imitate women, and women will imitate men. And those with private parts (ذوات الفروج) will ride saddles (السروج). Upon them, from my Ummah, is the curse of Allah." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! Indeed, at that time, the mosques (المساجد) will be decorated just as the synagogues (البيع) and churches (الكنائس) are decorated. The Masahif (المصاحف) will be adorned, the minarets (المنارات) will be lengthened, and the rows (الصفوف) will be numerous, but the hearts will be mutually hateful (متباغضة) and the tongues (السن) divergent." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! And at that time, the males (ذكور) of my Ummah will adorn themselves with gold (الذّهب), wear silk (الحرير) and brocade (الديباج), and use leopard skins (جلود النمور) as coverings (صفافاً)." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! And at that time, usury (الربا) will appear, and they will deal in al-inah (العينة) (1) and bribes (الرّشا). Religion (الدين) will be lowered, and the world (الدنيا) will be raised." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! And at that time, divorce (الطلاق) will become frequent, and no limit (حدّ) of Allah will be upheld, and they will not harm Allah in the least." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! And at that time, singing slave-girls (القينات) and musical instruments (المعازف) will appear, and the worst among my Ummah will rule over them." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! And at that time, the rich among my Ummah will perform Hajj (الحجّ) for pleasure (للنّزهة), the middle class for trade (للتجارة), and their poor for show and reputation (للرياء والسمعة). At that time, there will be people who learn the Quran but rush headlong after the world (الدنيا)." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! That is when the sacred things (المحارم) are violated, sins (المآثم) are committed, the wicked (الأشرار) are given authority over the righteous (الأخيار), lying (الكذب) becomes widespread, obstinacy (اللجاجة) appears, poverty (الفاقة) becomes widespread, they boast about clothing (اللباس), and it rains outside the proper season (أوان). They will approve of the drum (الكوبة) and musical instruments (المعازف), and they will reject commanding the right (الامر بالمعروف) and forbidding the wrong (النهي عن المنكر), until the believer in that time is more humiliated than a slave-girl (الأمة). Their reciters (قرّاؤهم) and worshippers (عبّادهم) will show mutual affection (التّلاؤم) among themselves, but those are the ones who are called the filthy, impure ones (الارجاس الانجاس) in the Kingdom of the Heavens (ملكوت السماوات)." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, the rich will not fear for the poor, until the beggar asks among the people between two Fridays and finds no one who places anything in his hand." Salman said: "And will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله?" He said: "Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, the Ruwaybidah (الرّويبضة) (2) will speak." Salman said: "What is the Ruwaybidah, O Messenger of Allah? May my father and mother be sacrificed for you!" He said: "He who speaks concerning the affairs of the general public (امر العامّة) is one who was not accustomed to speaking." Then they will not remain but a little while until the earth groans a groan (خورة), and every people will think only that it has groaned in their direction. Then they will remain for as long as Allah wills, and then, while they remain, the earth will cast forth the pieces of its liver (أفلاذ كبدها)." He said: "Gold and silver." Then He gestured with His hand towards the pillars (الأساطين) and said: "Like this. On that day, neither gold nor silver will be of benefit." This is the meaning of His saying: فَقَدْ جاءَ أَشْراطُها.

(1) Al-Inah (العينة), with a kasrah (بالكسر): The commodity (السلعة).

(2) The diminutive of al-Rabidah (الرابضة): He is the insignificant man (الرجل التافه).

(١٩) ** فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لِذَنْبِكَ ** (19) (So know that there is no god except Allah, and ask forgiveness for your sin) Meaning, if you know the bliss (سعادة) of the believers and the wretchedness (شقاوة) of the disbelievers, then remain firm upon what you possess of the knowledge of Divine Oneness (الوحدانية), and the perfection of the soul by rectifying its states and actions, and humbling it through seeking forgiveness for your sin. وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ ** (and for the believing men and the believing women) and for their sins, by supplicating for them and encouraging that which necessitates their forgiveness. وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ مُتَقَلَّبَكُمْ ** (and Allah knows your moving about) in the world (الدنيا), for it has stages that must be traversed, and your lodging (مَثْواكُمْ) in the Hereafter (العقبى), for it is the abode of your permanence.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار) and the saying of لا اله الّا الله (There is no god except Allah) are the best of worship." The Almighty, the Compeller (العزيز الجبّار), Allah said: فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لِذَنْبِكَ (So know that there is no god except Allah, and ask forgiveness for your sin).

** (٢٠) وَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَوْ لا نُزِّلَتْ سُورَةٌ ** (20) The faithful say, 'Why has not a surah been sent down?' Why has a surah not been sent down concerning the matter of al-Jihad (striving/fighting)?

فَإِذا أُنْزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ مُحْكَمَةٌ But when a decisive (مُحْكَمَةٌ) surah is sent down, made clear (مبيّنة), in which there is no ambiguity (لا تشابه فيها),

وَذُكِرَ فِيهَا الْقِتالُ and fighting (الْقِتالُ) is mentioned therein—that is, the command for it (الامر به)—

رَأَيْتَ الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ نَظَرَ الْمَغْشِيِّ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْمَوْتِ you see those in whose hearts is a sickness (مَرَضٌ) looking at you with the look of one who is fainting (الْمَغْشِيِّ عَلَيْهِ) due to death, out of cowardice (جبناً) and fear (ومخافة).

فَأَوْلى لَهُمْ ** So woe (ويل) to them.

(٢١) طاعَةٌ وَقَوْلٌ مَعْرُوفٌ ** (Obedience and a known word) is better for them. And it is narrated from Ubayy (ibn Ka'b) that he recited (it as): يقولون طاعَةٌ وَقَوْلٌ مَعْرُوفٌ فَإِذا عَزَمَ الْأَمْرُ ** (They say: Obedience and a known word. Then when the matter is resolved). Meaning: (when the matter) becomes serious (جدّ). The resolve (عزم) of the people of the matter (أصحاب الامر) was attributed to the matter (الامر) metaphorically (مجازاً), and its answer (apodosis) is omitted (محذوف). فَلَوْ صَدَقُوا اللهَ ** (So if they were truthful to Allah) Meaning: regarding what they claimed concerning their eagerness for الجهاد (striving), the truthfulness would have been خَيْراً لَهُمْ** (better for them).

(٢٢) فَهَلْ عَسَيْتُمْ ** (22) ** فَهَلْ عَسَيْتُمْ ** Is it then expected of you, if you take charge of the affairs of the people and become rulers over them, or if you turn away and abandon Islam, ** أَنْ تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَتُقَطِّعُوا أَرْحامَكُمْ ** by vying with one another for authority and pulling it towards yourselves, or by returning to what you were upon in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهليّة) of mutual raiding and fighting with kin (الارقاب)? And the meaning is that they, due to their weakness in religion and their eagerness for the world (الدنيا), are deserving that this be expected of them by whoever knows their condition, and He says to them: ** فَهَلْ عَسَيْتُمْ. And it was read (قُرِئَ) as تولّيتم (if you take charge), meaning: if oppressors (ظلمة) take charge over you, you would go out with them and assist them in corruption (الإفساد) and cutting off kinship (قطيعة الرّحم).

And in al-Majma', this reading (القراءة) is attributed to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

And in al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): that it was revealed concerning Banu Umayyah.

(٢٣) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَعَنَهُمُ اللهُ فَأَصَمَّهُمْ وَأَعْمى أَبْصارَهُمْ ** (23) They are the ones whom Allah has cursed, so He has made them deaf and blinded their sight. [He made them deaf] from listening to the truth, وَأَعْمى أَبْصارَهُمْ ** (and blinded their sight) so they are not guided to His path. (٢٤) أَفَلا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ. (24) Do they not contemplate the Quran? In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq and al-Kazim (peace be upon them both): It means: أَفَلا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ ** (Do they not contemplate the Quran?) so that they fulfill the right (الحقّ) that is incumbent upon them, أَمْ عَلى قُلُوبٍ أَقْفالُها ** (Or are there locks on the hearts?) No remembrance (ذكر) reaches them, nor is any matter (امر) revealed to them. And the attribution (إضافة) of the locks (الاقفال) to them [the hearts] is to indicate locks (أقفال) that are appropriate for them, specific to them, and do not resemble the customary locks (الاقفال المعهودة). In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, you have a heart (قلباً) and hearing organs (مسامع). And indeed, when Allah desires to guide a servant, He opens the hearing organs (مسامع) of his heart. And when He desires something other than that for him, He seals the hearing organs (مسامع) of his heart, so he will never be reformed (يصلح أبداً). And this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: أَمْ عَلى قُلُوبٍ أَقْفالُها. (Or are there locks on the hearts?)"

(٢٥) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ ارْتَدُّوا عَلى أَدْبارِهِمْ ** (25) Indeed those who turn their backs to what they were upon of disbelief (الكفر) مِنْ بَعْدِ ما تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الْهُدَى الشَّيْطانُ سَوَّلَ لَهُمْ ** after the guidance has become manifest to them—Satan has tempted them made easy for them وَأَمْلى لَهُمْ ** and given them false hopes. It was said: and extended for them in hopes (الآمال) and desires (الأماني), and another meaning for it will come later. And it was read: واملى لهم (and He gave them respite), meaning: and I give them respite (أُمْلِي لَهُمْ), meaning: I grant them delay (أُمْهِلُهُمْ). And أُمْلِي is based on the passive structure (على البناء للمفعول).

(٢٦) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قالُوا لِلَّذِينَ كَرِهُوا ما نَزَّلَ اللهُ سَنُطِيعُكُمْ فِي بَعْضِ الْأَمْرِ وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ إِسْرارَهُمْ ** (26) That is because they said to those who were averse to what Allah has sent down, 'We will obey you in some matters,' and Allah knows their secrets. And it was recited as a masdar (المصدر).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "So-and-so and so-and-so apostatized (ارتدّا) from faith by abandoning the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)." He said: "By Allah, it was revealed concerning them both and their followers. And it is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, which Gabriel brought down to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله: ** ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قالُوا لِلَّذِينَ كَرِهُوا ما نَزَّلَ اللهُ ** concerning Ali سَنُطِيعُكُمْ فِي بَعْضِ الْأَمْرِ ." He said: "They invited Banu Umayyah (بني أميّة) to their covenant (ميثاقهم) that they would not allow the authority (الأمر) to be among us after the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and that they would not give us anything from the Khums (الخمس). They said: 'If we give it to them, they will not need anything and will not care if the authority is not among them.' So they said: 'We will obey you in some of the matter (الأمر) to which you invited us, which is the Khums, that we will not give them anything from it.' And 'what Allah has sent down' is what He obligated upon His creation concerning the wilayah of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). Abu Ubaydah was with them, and he was their scribe (كاتبهم). Then Allah sent down: **أَمْ أَبْرَمُوا أَمْراً فَإِنَّا مُبْرِمُونَ أَمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّا لا نَسْمَعُ سِرَّهُمْ وَنَجْواهُمْ ** the verse."

And al-Qummi narrated what is similar in meaning, with additions and omissions.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding: الشَّيْطانُ سَوَّلَ لَهُمْ ** (The devil has embellished for them), meaning the second one (الثاني).

And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them both): That they are Banu Umayyah (بنو أميّة) who were averse to what Allah sent down concerning the wilayah of Ali (peace be upon him).

** فَكَيْفَ إِذا تَوَفَّتْهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ ** (27) So how will it be when the angels take them away, So how will they act and scheme when, at that time, يَضْرِبُونَ وُجُوهَهُمْ وَأَدْبارَهُمْ (they strike their faces and their backs)?

** ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمُ اتَّبَعُوا ما أَسْخَطَ اللهَ وَكَرِهُوا رِضْوانَهُ فَأَحْبَطَ أَعْمالَهُمْ ** (28) That is because they followed what displeased Allah and were averse to His pleasure, so He rendered their works void. Because of that.

In Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: They were averse to Ali, whose guardianship (ولايته) Allah commanded on the Day of Badr, the Day of Hunayn, at Batin Nakhla, the Day of al-Tarwiyah, and the Day of Arafah. Fifteen verses were revealed concerning him during the Pilgrimage (الحجّة) in which the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was prevented from entering the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام), and at al-Juhfa and at Ghadir Khumm. And al-Qummi (said): ما أَسْخَطَ اللهَ ** (what displeased Allah) means the allegiance (موالاة) to so-and-so and so-and-so, and the oppressors of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). فَأَحْبَطَ اللهُ أَعْمالَهُمْ ** (so He rendered their works void) means the good deeds (الخيرات) which they performed.

(٢٩) أَمْ حَسِبَ الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ أَنْ لَنْ يُخْرِجَ اللهُ أَضْغانَهُمْ ** (29) Do those in whose hearts is a sickness suppose that Allah will not expose their malice? That Allah will not expose their hatreds (أحقادهم) to His Messenger and the believers. (٣٠) وَلَوْ نَشاءُ لَأَرَيْناكَهُمْ ** (30) Had We wished, We would have shown them to you, We would have made them known to you by signs (دلائل) through which you would recognize them individually (بأعيانهم) فَلَعَرَفْتَهُمْ بِسِيماهُمْ by their marks (علاماتهم) with which We brand them (نسمهم بها) وَلَتَعْرِفَنَّهُمْ فِي لَحْنِ الْقَوْلِ ** in its style (أسلوبه), its inclination (امالته) towards a certain direction (جهة), and its ambiguity (تورية).

In al-Amali, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: I spoke four words, and Allah Almighty sent down confirmation of them in His Book. I said: 'A person is hidden beneath his tongue, so when he speaks, he becomes manifest.' Then He sent down: وَلَتَعْرِفَنَّهُمْ فِي لَحْنِ الْقَوْلِ (And you will surely recognize them by their tone of speech).

And in al-Majma', narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, he said: The لَحْنِ الْقَوْلِ ** (tone of speech) is their hatred (بغضهم) of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). He said: And we used to recognize the hypocrites (المنافقين) during the time of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم by their hatred of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). He said: And the like of this has been narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari.

And from Ubadah ibn al-Samit, he said: We used to test (نبوّر) our children by the love of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). If we saw one of them who did not love him, we knew that he was not of sound origin (لغير رشده). Anas said: No hypocrite (منافق) remained hidden during the time of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم after this Ayah. وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ أَعْمالَكُمْ ** (And Allah knows your deeds.) So He will recompense you according to your intention (قصدكم), since deeds are judged by intentions (النيّات).

(٣١) وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ (And We will surely test you) by commanding al-Jihad (الجهاد) and all other difficult obligations (التكاليف الشاقة). حَتَّى نَعْلَمَ الْمُجاهِدِينَ مِنْكُمْ وَالصَّابِرِينَ ** (so that We may ascertain the strivers among you and the patient ones) regarding their difficulties (مشاقّها), and We will test your reports (أَخْبارَكُمْ) concerning your faith (ايمانكم) and your loyalty (موالاتكم) to the believers, regarding their truthfulness and falsehood. And the three verbs (الافعال الثلاثة) were read with the letter yā' (الياء) so that they conform to what precedes them. And he attributed this (reading) in al-Majma' (المجمع) to al-Baqir (peace be upon him) as well. And ونبلو was read with the sukun (سكون) on the wāw (الواو), meaning: and We (ونحن) test.

(32) ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ (32) Indeed, those who disbelieve and bar [others] from the way of Allah, Al-Qummi said, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): وَشَاقُّوا الرَّسُولَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الْهُدى ** and defy the Apostle after the guidance has become manifest to them, He (peace be upon him) said: They cut him off regarding his Household (أهل بيته) after He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) had taken the covenant from them for him. ** لَنْ يَضُرُّوا اللهَ شَيْئاً ** shall not harm Allah in the least, by their disbelief and their hindering, وَسَيُحْبِطُ أَعْمالَهُمْ. and He will render their works void.

** (٣٣) ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَلا تُبْطِلُوا أَعْمالَكُمْ. (33) O you who have faith! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and do not render your works void.

In Thawab al-A'mal (في ثواب الأعمال), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) said: "Whoever says سبحان الله (Glory be to Allah), Allah plants for him a tree in Paradise by means of it. And whoever says الحمد لله (All praise belongs to Allah), Allah plants for him a tree in Paradise by means of it. And whoever says لا اله الّا الله (There is no god except Allah), Allah plants for him a tree in Paradise by means of it. And whoever says الله أكبر (Allah is the Greatest), Allah plants for him a tree in Paradise by means of it." Then a man from Quraysh said: "O Messenger of Allah, our trees in Paradise are indeed numerous!" He said: "Yes, but beware lest you send fires upon them and burn them down. And that is because Allah the Almighty says: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَلا تُبْطِلُوا أَعْمالَكُمْ. (O you who have faith! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and do not render your works void.)"

(٣٤) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ ثُمَّ ماتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَهُمْ. (34) Indeed those who are faithless and bar [others] from the way of Allah, and then die as faithless ones—Allah will never forgive them. (٣٥) فَلا تَهِنُوا So do not weaken (فلا تضعفوا) وَتَدْعُوا إِلَى السَّلْمِ and do not call to peace (الصُّلح) out of cowardice (خورا) and humiliation (وتذلّلاً). And it was recited with the kasrah on the sīn (بكسر السين). وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ while you are the superior ones, [meaning] the dominant ones (الأغلبون). وَاللهُ مَعَكُمْ and Allah is with you, [meaning] He is your Helper (ناصركم). وَلَنْ يَتِرَكُمْ أَعْمالَكُمْ and He will never deprive you of [the reward of] your deeds, [meaning] He will never waste your deeds (ولن يضيع أعمالكم). [This is derived] from (وترت الرجل) (I deprived the man) when you kill a relative (متعلّقاً) of his, whether a close relative (قريب) or a dear one (حميم), and thus isolate him (فأفردته عنه) from the relative (الوتر). The nullification of the reward of the deed (تعطيل ثواب العمل) and its isolation (افراده منه) from the doer is likened to this. And this verse is abrogating (ناسخة) the Almighty's saying: وَإِنْ جَنَحُوا لِلسَّلْمِ فَاجْنَحْ لَها ** (And if they incline to peace, incline to it too), as previously mentioned (كما مرّ).

(٣٦) ** إِنَّمَا الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ** (36) The life of the world is only play and diversion having no permanence. وَإِنْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَتَتَّقُوا يُؤْتِكُمْ أُجُورَكُمْ ** If you believe and are wary [of Allah], He will give you your rewards, the reward for your faith and your piety. وَلا يَسْئَلْكُمْ أَمْوالَكُمْ and He will not ask you for your wealth, all your wealth, rather He restricts Himself to a small portion, such as the tenth (العشر), or half of the tenth (نصف العشر), or a quarter of the tenth (ربع العشر).

(٣٧) ** إِنْ يَسْئَلْكُمُوها فَيُحْفِكُمْ ** (37) If He asks you for it, and presses you, meaning, He would distress you by demanding the entirety of it. الإحفاء (al-iḥfāʾ) means exaggeration and reaching the limit. تَبْخَلُوا ** you will be stingy, so you would not give. وَيُخْرِجْ أَضْغانَكُمْ ** and He will expose your malice. Al-Qummi said: [It means] the enmity (العداوة) that is in your chests. (٣٨) ** ها أَنْتُمْ هؤُلاءِ ** (38) Ah! You are those It was said: meaning, you, O addressed ones, are these described people. And Al-Qummi said its meaning is: You, O these people. تُدْعَوْنَ لِتُنْفِقُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** who are called upon to spend in the way of Allah; This includes the expenditure for fighting (الغزو), al-Zakat (الزكاة), and other things. فَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يَبْخَلُ ** yet among you are those who are stingy. [Meaning] people who are stingy. وَمَنْ يَبْخَلْ فَإِنَّما يَبْخَلُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ ** And whoever is stingy is stingy only toward his own soul. For the benefit of spending (الإنفاق) and the harm of withholding (الإمساك) return to him. وَاللهُ الْغَنِيُّ وَأَنْتُمُ الْفُقَراءُ ** Allah is the All-sufficient, and you are the needy. Thus, what He commands you with is due to your need. If you comply, it is for your benefit, and if you turn away, it is against you. وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا ** And if you turn away, This is connected to وَإِنْ تُؤْمِنُوا. (And if you believe) [from a previous verse]. Al-Qummi said: [It means] turning away from the guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). He will replace you with another people, meaning, He will establish other people in your place. Al-Qummi said: He will admit them into this matter. ثُمَّ لا يَكُونُوا أَمْثالَكُمْ ** then they will not be like you. He said: [They will not be like you] in your enmity (معاداتكم), opposition (خلافكم), and oppression (ظلمكم) toward the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He meant the sons of the freed clients (الموالي المعتقين).

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: إِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا ** (If you turn away), O assembly of Arabs, يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْماً غَيْرَكُمْ ** (He will replace you with another people), meaning the clients (الموالي).

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: By Allah, He has replaced them with better people than them: the clients (الموالي). And concerning this [verse], it is narrated: Indeed, some people among the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Messenger of Allah, who are these people whom Allah mentioned in His Book?" Salman was beside the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so He struck His hand upon Salman's thigh and said: "This man and his people. By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, even if faith (الإيمان) were tied to the Pleiades (الثّريا), men from Persia (فارس) would attain it."

In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites the Surah al-Ladhina Kafaru (Surah Muhammad) will never doubt, and doubt will never enter his religion, and Allah the Almighty will never afflict him with poverty, nor fear of a ruler (سلطان), and he will remain protected from doubt (الشكّ) and disbelief (الكفر) forever until he dies. When he dies, Allah will assign a thousand angels to him in his grave who will pray in his grave, and the reward of their prayers will be for him. They will escort him until they make him stand in the station of the secure (موقف الآمن) before Allah the Almighty, and he will be in the security (أمان) of Allah and the security of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

And in al-Majma', the like of it is narrated with a slight difference.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever wishes to know our state and the state of our enemies, let him recite Surah Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, for he will see in it an Ayah (آية) concerning us and an Ayah (آية) concerning them.
